response:
We are given the matrix:

$$
A = \begin{bmatrix}
-5 & -5 & 5 & 1 \\
-3 & -3 & -4 & -1 \\
-4 & 1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We are to find the **determinant** of this 4x4 matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use the **cofactor expansion** (Laplace expansion) along the first row.

The determinant of a 4x4 matrix can be computed using the formula:

$$
\det(A) = \sum_{j=1}^{4} (-1)^{i+j} a_{ij} \cdot \det(M_{ij})
$$

Where $ M_{ij} $ is the minor matrix obtained by removing row $ i $ and column $ j $.

We'll compute the determinant using the first row:

$$
\det(A) = a_{11} \cdot \det(M_{11}) - a_{12} \cdot \det(M_{12}) + a_{13} \cdot \det(M_{13}) - a_{14} \cdot \det(M_{14})
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute each minor

#### Minor $ M_{11} $: remove row 1, column 1

$$
M_{11} = \begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -1 \\
1 & 1 & 4 \\
2 & 4 & 4
\end{bmatrix}
$$

$$
\det(M_{11}) = -3 \cdot (1 \cdot 4 - 4 \cdot 4) - (-4) \cdot (1 \cdot 4 - 4 \cdot 2) + (-1) \cdot (1 \cdot 4 - 1 \cdot 2)
$$

$$
= -3 \cdot (4 - 16) + 4 \cdot (4 - 8) - 1 \cdot (4 - 2)
= -3 \cdot (-12) + 4 \cdot (-4) - 1 \cdot 2
= 36 - 16 - 2 = 18
$$

---
